hero.title: Academic formation in ITICs.
hero.subtitle: Review the objective, graduate profile, competencies, and study areas that shape the Information and Communications Technologies Engineering program at ITSOEH.

objective.title: Objective
objective.body: Train professionals capable of developing, integrating, and managing information and communications technologies to improve organizational productivity.

profile.title: Graduate profile
profile.body: Competencies in networks, software, databases, security, communication systems, technology projects, and emerging technologies.

curriculum.title: ITIC-2010-225 curriculum
curriculum.body: Semester-based study plan with training in programming, networks, telecommunications, databases, management, and research.

residency.title: Professional residency
residency.body: Partnership with companies and institutions where students apply their competencies in real-world scenarios.

mission.title: Mission
mission.body: Train professionals who are competent in designing, developing, and implementing innovative solutions in information and communications technologies, under international standards and certifications, to meet social and industry needs.

vision.title: Vision
vision.body: Be a consolidated program recognized for educational quality, technology services, certified faculty, and certified labs, focused on cutting-edge technology to solve social and productive challenges in the region.

generalObjective.title: General objective
generalObjective.body: Train professionals capable of developing, integrating, and managing information and communications technologies that contribute to organizational productivity, with leadership, critical thinking, ethics, entrepreneurial vision, and commitment to sustainable development.

software.title: Programming and software development
software.body: Programming foundations, object-oriented programming, web programming, mobile development, and software engineering.

networks.title: Networks and telecommunications
networks.body: Network fundamentals, computer networks, telecommunications, emerging networks, wireless technologies, and network security.

databases.title: Databases and systems
databases.body: Database fundamentals, database workshops, distributed databases, and operating systems.

math.title: Mathematics and modeling
math.body: Calculus, linear algebra, probability, discrete mathematics, decision-making, and communications.

iot.title: Hardware, architecture, and IoT
iot.body: Electricity, circuits, computer architecture, connected devices, and embedded systems.

management.title: Management, research, and business
management.body: Management, projects, research, e-business, entrepreneurship, and sustainable development.

networks.title: Networks and telecommunications
networks.body: Design, implementation, and management of computer networks, communications, and connected infrastructure.

software.title: Software and information systems
software.body: Application and system development for process management, decision-making, and organizational operations.

databases.title: Databases
databases.body: Design, administration, and management of secure, available, and reliable databases.

security.title: IT security and auditing
security.body: Implementation of controls, policies, and processes to protect information and evaluate technology systems.

embedded.title: Embedded systems and IoT
embedded.body: Integration of hardware, software, sensors, and devices for purpose-built applications.

management.title: Management and entrepreneurship
management.body: Technology project management, innovation, consulting, and digital solution creation.

curriculum.title: ITIC-2010-225 curriculum.
curriculum.body: The curriculum is organized across nine semesters and integrates core training, professional training, specialization, social service, complementary activities, and professional residency.
curriculum.semester1: 1st semester: programming foundations, mathematics, and ethics.
curriculum.semester2: 2nd semester: object-oriented programming, algebra, and statistics.
curriculum.semester3: 3rd semester: data structures, databases, and management.
curriculum.semester4: 4th semester: networks, programming II, circuits, and software engineering.
curriculum.semester5: 5th semester: networks, communications, distributed databases, and projects.
curriculum.semester6: 6th semester: web, telecommunications, operating systems, and wireless technologies.
curriculum.semester7: 7th semester: mobile development, emerging networks, HCI, and research.
curriculum.semester8: 8th semester: security, auditing, knowledge engineering, and e-business.

complementary.title: Complementary academic experience.
complementary.body: In addition to academic formation, ITICs includes complementary activities, projects, workshops, visits, clubs, and collaborative experiences that strengthen leadership, teamwork, communication, and professional vision. It is also reinforced through official academies, certifications, technical clubs, and community activities.

cta.title: See how this formation becomes real projects.
cta.admissionTitle: Ready to start your path in ITICs?
